fact: September 26 sits exactly 269 days into the year, which means there are only 96 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this date falls in the middle of autumn's arrival - leaves are typically changing colors rapidly right now!
September 26 often marks peak spider season in northern climates when they're searching for warm places to spend winter!
The sun rises and sets at nearly the same time all around the world on this date because we just passed the autumn equinox a few days ago.
Squirrels are typically in full preparation mode around September 26, often burying up to 25 nuts per day to prepare for winter in North America!
In the Southern Hemisphere, September 26 is when spring flowers like wattle trees often explode into bright yellow blooms.
This date falls right in the middle of Libra season in the zodiac, when the scales are balanced - just like day and night were a few days ago at the equinox!
Monarch butterflies are typically flying over 2,000 miles south right now, and September 26 is when massive swarms often pass through the central United States on their journey to Mexico.
Apple trees usually hit their peak ripeness right around this date in northern regions - that's why apple picking season is in full swing!
September 26 marks when northern countries start losing daylight quickly - they'll typically lose about 3 minutes of sunlight every day from now until winter.
Geese are often honking overhead because this date falls right in the middle of their great migration south in North America - some flocks contain over 1,000 birds!
